sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Terminology 33:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Terminology 33:1
items.html: Growing coarser is considered a “descent,” meaning having descended from its degree. Refinement means an “ascent,” since it ascended in equivalence of form with Ein Sof. The rule is that the greater the refinement, the higher it is, and the greater the coarseness, the lower it is.
